nodes.label: Rich parse path (ThermalZone + inline ThermalBoundary present)
nodes.norm_label: rich parse path (thermalzone + inline thermalboundary present)

nodes.label: 3-tier construction resolver (layered / uValue / archetype)
nodes.rationale: Resolve each referenced construction id in priority order (1. layered materials, 2. U-value only, 3. archetype lookup) so one code path handles buildings regardless of how much construction detail is present in the source data.

nodes.label: Adjacency enrichment mechanism (coplanar+overlap surface matching)
nodes.rationale: Detect shared/party walls by matching boundary-surface polygons that are coplanar (anti-parallel normals, plane distance < tol) and overlap in projected area, since the source GML carries no explicit inter-zone adjacency links.

nodes.label: Intermediate model / abstraction seam (BuildingModel)
nodes.rationale: model.py is the single abstraction seam: parse_gml.py normalizes both rich and poor GML inputs into it, and idf_writer.py only ever consumes this model — decoupling CityGML/EnergyADE parsing quirks from IDF-emission logic.

nodes.label: Detail-agnostic hard requirement
nodes.rationale: Hard requirement that the same code path handle buildings with full layered materials, U-value-only data, or only an archetype code, rather than special-casing the converter per dataset richness.

nodes.label: Stack decision: Python + lxml + direct-text IDF
nodes.rationale: Chose plain f-string IDF templates over eppy/geomeppy to avoid an IDD dependency and keep the writer simple and fast.
nodes.norm_label: stack decision: python + lxml + direct-text idf

nodes.label: Archetype table decision: scaffold CSV now, swap Excel later
nodes.rationale: Scaffold data/archetypes.csv with placeholder U-values behind a loader that also accepts .xlsx, so the pipeline is runnable end-to-end immediately and real national/TABULA data can replace it later with no code change.
nodes.norm_label: archetype table decision: scaffold csv now, swap excel later

nodes.label: Output decision: minimal valid runnable IDF (V1 scope stop)
nodes.rationale: V1 emits only the objects needed for a minimal valid, loadable IDF covering geometry + constructions + glazings, explicitly stopping short of HVAC/loads/schedules to keep scope tight and directly verifiable in EnergyPlus.
nodes.norm_label: output decision: minimal valid runnable idf (v1 scope stop)

nodes.label: Adjacency decision: decoupled standalone script
nodes.rationale: enrich_adjacency.py is a separate, fully decoupled preprocessor (own CLI, no imports from the converter) so its adjacency-matching logic can be lifted into another repo; the converter reads injected gen: attributes if present, else falls back to type-based defaults.

nodes.label: V1 out-of-scope: HVAC/loads/schedules (per PLAN.md)
nodes.rationale: PLAN.md Sec.0 and Sec.12 explicitly exclude HVAC, internal loads, schedules, occupancy, DHW and infiltration from V1 scope ('scope stops at geometry + constructions + glazings'). Verified against current src/idf_writer.py: write_idf() calls _write_zone_hvac() per zone and _write_outputs(), i.e. ZoneHVAC:IdealLoadsAirSystem + heating/cooling output variables are already implemented and shipped, contradicting the PLAN's stated V1 scope boundary — likely scope grew past the original plan without the plan being updated.

nodes.label: archetypes.csv schema per README.md (use_type,period,u_wall,u_roof,u_floor,u_window) — verified NOT present in actual data/archetypes.csv header or src/archetypes.py column names
nodes.norm_label: archetypes.csv schema per readme.md (use_type,period,u_wall,u_roof,u_floor,u_window) — verified not present in actual data/archetypes.csv header or src/archetypes.py column names

nodes.label: geometry.py responsibility per PLAN.md: posList parse, local origin, outward-normal orientation
nodes.norm_label: geometry.py responsibility per plan.md: poslist parse, local origin, outward-normal orientation

nodes.norm_label: geometry.py responsibility per readme.md: 'polygon simplification utilities'
